questions.text: Error correction: I've lived in Barcelona since six years.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: This option suggests replacing "since" with "has" and saying, "I've lived in Barcelona has six years." However, this change is not appropriate. "Has" does not convey the intended meaning and is not a suitable replacement for "since."
This option suggests replacing "since" with "been" and saying, "I've lived in Barcelona been six years." However, this change is not correct. "Been" does not fit grammatically in this context.

This option suggests replacing "since" with "at" and saying, "I've lived in Barcelona at six years." However, this change does not accurately convey the intended meaning. Using "at" in this context is not appropriate.
This option suggests replacing "since" with "has been" and saying, "I've lived in Barcelona has been six years." This option is incorrect because "has been" is not used correctly here.

This option suggests replacing "since" with "for" and saying, "I've lived in Barcelona for six years." This is the most suitable and correct option. Using "for" indicates the duration of time (six years) that you have lived in Barcelona.

questions.explanation: For is the correct word to be used here in place of since, thus option E is the correct answer. questions.text: Figure out the error: Scientist now hope that cloning can successfully be conducted in human beings in the near future.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: The subject "Scientist" should be pluralized to "Scientists" to match the verb "hope." The error is present in Options B.
